title: Cyclobothra plummerae
taxonomy: Cyclobothra plummerae is a species of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. It is native to Western Australia. The classification of this plant includes being a part of the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. This species is further categorized into the class Magnoliopsida, and the order Proteales. The classification of Cyclobothra plummerae is also known as being in the family Proteaceae, subfamily Proteoideae.

intro: Cyclobothra plummerae, also known as Cyclocheiloplera plummerae, is a plant species within the family Menispermaceae, commonly referred to as the moonseed family. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclobothra plummerae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cyclobothra plummerae is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is commonly used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
